competition: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Group C · MetLife Stadium

items.title: Vini's goal puts him in Ronaldinho company
items.text: That equaliser wasn't just pretty — it means Vini Jr has now scored at two different World Cups, matching a mark held by Ronaldinho Gaúcho in the famous yellow shirt. Big-stage player doing big-stage things. 💛💚

items.title: Bono is standing in Brazil's way
items.text: Igor Thiago thought he'd won it early in the second half off a quick Vini throw, but Bounou flung himself across to deny the turnaround. Morocco's keeper is having a night — he's the main reason this is still level. Someone find a way past him. 🧤

items.title: Brazil come out swinging after the break
items.text: Whatever Ancelotti said at the interval worked — the Seleção look sharper, pressing higher and the likelier side to nick a winner. Keep this tempo and the three points are right there. Just need that final ball. 💛💚

items.title: Morocco's TikTok anthem is everywhere: 'Bono! Hakimi! Diop!'
items.text: Credit where it's due — Morocco's unofficial Cup anthem (chanting Bono, Hakimi, Issa Diop) has blown past a million views and 65k videos. Properly catchy. We'll let them have the song... we'll take the football. 😏

items.title: Carlo warms up Endrick... then sends on someone else
items.text: The whole bench was screaming for Endrick — Ancelotti even had him warming up — but on came Luiz Henrique (for Igor Thiago) and Matheus Cunha (for Paquetá) instead. Brazilian Twitter is in flames. The kid's moment will have to wait. For now. 😤

items.title: Why the Endrick clamor? He won the last warm-up
items.text: The 'VEM, ENDRICK' chorus isn't random — in Brazil's final pre-Cup test on June 6, the kid came off the bench and scored to beat Egypt. With it stuck at 1-1, the whole country wants that same super-sub magic right now. Carlo, the bench is calling. ⚡

items.title: The numbers: a genuine heavyweight scrap
items.text: The xG says it's even-stevens with bite: Morocco edging the big chances (1.22 to 0.85) while Brazil keep the ball (54%). And keep an eye on Casemiro — booked on 37', he's now walking a yellow-card tightrope in midfield. Tense, but proud: the Seleção standing toe-to-toe with the world's No.7. 💪

items.title: Neymar breaks the internet from the sidelines
items.text: He's not in the squad, but the king still owns the timeline — Neymar's emotional pre-match post ('thank you for letting me live once again') went viral, on top of that teary squad-day call with bestie Raphinha. Watching this one hurt from afar. We miss you, Ney. 👑

items.title: The chant war: 'Go Morocco' vs 'CONTA, CONTA'
items.text: Over in Times Square the two fanbases went to war on the mic — Morocco chanting 'Go Morocco', and Brazil firing straight back with 'conta, conta' (count the five stars on the badge) and 'mil gols, mil gols, Brasil'. Petty, loud, glorious. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

items.title: Crowd cranks up 'Sou brasileiro' when it got tense
items.text: When Morocco had Brazil under the cosh, the green-and-yellow corner answered the only way it knows — a full-throated 'Sou brasileiro, com muito orgulho, com muito amor.' Goosebumps in New Jersey. This support never folds. 🇧🇷

items.title: Rio is watching: 10,000 deep on Copacabana
items.text: Back home, the Arena Copacabana packed up to 10,000 fans in front of giant screens on the sand for the opener. Beach, 30°C sun, and the Seleção to roar on — peak Brazil. 🏖️

items.title: The A-list is out: Anitta, Katy Perry, LISA, Future
items.text: The Cup's opening spectacle pulled Anitta, Katy Perry, LISA and Future, and the stands are dotted with famous faces. Brazil always brings the show. ✨

items.title: Vini 'vanished' from Virginia's Instagram right before kickoff
items.text: Hours before the opener, eagle-eyed fans noticed Vini Jr had disappeared from Virginia's Instagram — and the web melted. Then he goes and scores the equaliser. Petty? Iconic? Both. The breakup arc is writing itself. 👻

items.title: Igor Thiago and the miss that broke Brazilian hearts
items.text: Vini whipped in a gorgeous cross and Igor Thiago somehow headed it to row Z. The timeline went full 'PELO AMOR DE DEUS.' We love him, but that one's going straight in the blooper reel. 🙈

items.title: Meanwhile outside: 'Honk if you love geese'
items.text: On the road to the MetLife, a dozen protesters turned up with handmade signs — 'Honk if you love geese' and 'No to gassing geese.' World Cup chaos has no offseason. Fans drove past absolutely baffled. 🪿

items.title: VINIIIII! Equaliser of pure spite — 1-1!
items.text: Bruno Guimarães fed him on the left, Vini left El Aynaoui for dead, cut inside and rifled it past Bounou. GAME ON. And remember — his ex Virginia is on the Globo broadcast watching him do it. The man scores when it matters AND when it stings. 💛💚

items.title: The whole timeline is screaming: VEM, ENDRICK!
items.text: Brazil went a goal down and the internet made up its mind — get the kid off the bench. With it level at 1-1, every Brazilian group chat wants Endrick on to go win it. Carlo, are you reading this? 👀

items.title: Ancelotti's pre-match quote is aging like milk
items.text: "The World Cup is won by whoever concedes the least, not whoever scores the most," said Carlo... and then Brazil shipped one on 19'. Vini's bailed him out for now — but the pundits already had the clip cued up. Relax, the man's won everything. Probably. 😅

items.title: WAG drama: Raphinha accused of being 'cold' on camera
items.text: The doc 'Convocadas' (about the Seleção's wives & girlfriends) has Brazil hooked. Fans piled on Raphinha for seeming cold to wife Natália Belloni — she fired straight back: "I'm treated like a princess." Mid-World Cup soap, exactly as ordered. 🍿

items.title: Morocco's secret weapon: Ancelotti knows them too well
items.text: Half of Morocco's engine room is Serie A-flavoured (Saibari, El Aynaoui) — ironic against an Italian-coached Brazil. Surprise call: El Khannouss got the left flank ahead of Hakimi as winger, with Achraf bombing on from full-back. Crafty. Annoyingly crafty. 😤

items.title: Morocco grab an early one — relax, it's nothing
items.text: Saibari poked one in early and Morocco went in front. We said breathe — the Seleção have 90 minutes and the film had barely started. Vini levelled it before the break. Far from over. 💛💚

items.title: Virginia is covering the Cup... a week after the Vini split
items.text: Talked round by Luciano Huck and Angélica, Virginia Fonseca kept her Domingão slot and is in New York covering World Cup behind-the-scenes — right at her ex's opener. Him on the pitch, her on the broadcast. It's a soap opera inside the Cup. 👀
